title: lichen_l2_recv function exists comment could be clearer
description:
In lichen_l2.c:259-269, the comment explains why lichen_l2_recv exists:

```c
/*
 * WHY THIS EXISTS (even though it just returns NET_CONTINUE):
 * Zephyr's net_if_recv_data() unconditionally calls iface->l2->recv() without
 * a NULL check. If we omit this callback from NET_L2_INIT, any packet injection
 * via net_recv_data() would dereference NULL and crash. This function must exist
 * even when it performs no L2-specific processing. Compare Zephyr's dummy L2
 * (subsys/net/l2/dummy/dummy.c) which follows the same pattern.
 */
```

This is good defensive documentation. However, the function name `lichen_l2_recv` could mislead readers into thinking it does receiving, when actually it is the callback invoked AFTER a packet has been received and before it goes to the IP layer.

What goes wrong: Someone searching for "where does RX happen" might look at this function and be confused when the actual RX path is in `lichen_l2_input`.

Suggested fix: Consider renaming to `lichen_l2_rx_filter` or `lichen_l2_pkt_intercept`, or add a one-liner clarifying the call context: "Called by net_recv_data() after lichen_l2_input() injects a packet."
close_reason: No change needed. The existing docstring and inline comment are already clear: explains WHY the function exists (Zephyr requires non-NULL l2->recv callback), references the source (net_if_recv_data), compares to Zephyr's dummy L2 pattern, and explains the return value rationale. Comment quality is good as-is.
